name: Kentucky Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Kentucky - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Kentucky Board of Licensure and Certification for Dietitians and Nutritionists

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Must show your conferred master's degree. Can be sent securely via Clearinghouse to the board.

requiredDocuments.name: Supervised Practice Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation showing completion of at least 1,000 hours.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Kentucky dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The application fee for initial licensure as a dietitian (or dual licensure as a dietitian/nutritionist) is $50.

mainEntity.name: When does the Kentucky license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Kentucky dietitian licenses expire annually on October 31st. The renewal fee is $50. There is a 60-day grace period (until Dec 31st) with late fees applied.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements?

acceptedAnswer.text: Licensees must complete at least 15 hours of approved continuing education every year prior to the October 31st renewal deadline.

mainEntity.name: Does Kentucky have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Kentucky. To work in Kentucky, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Kentucky?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Kentucky while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
